mechanism_narrative: A sustained low and stable federal funds rate indicates accommodative monetary conditions, reducing the cost of capital and supporting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:low interest rates generally support equity markets
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 consistently produces negative 20-day returns when FEDFUNDS is below 2.5% over a 2-year period, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and potential economic contraction, leading to investor pessimism and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE increases by >0.5pp over 3 months, but the S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances over a 5-year period, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in both GDP growth and housing starts indicates a broad-based contraction in economic demand, leading to reduced corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate is negative and HOUST declines by more than 10% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:housing is a leading indicator of economic health
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth is negative and HOUST declines by >10% MoM, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in >40% of such events over a 3-year period, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation, a persistent increase in the Consumer Price Index signals ongoing price pressures, forcing the central bank to maintain tight monetary policy, which negatively impacts equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:high inflation often leads to higher interest rates
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL annual growth is >3% but S&P 500 5-day returns are consistently positive (Rank IC > 0.05) in this regime over a 2-year period, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales indicate robust consumer spending, which translates to higher corporate revenues and profits, driving positive sentiment and S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending is a major component of GDP
failure_condition: If RSAFS MoM growth > 0.5% but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year period, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing and supply-side constraints or reduced demand for goods, signaling an economic slowdown that negatively impacts corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by more than 1% over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:industrial production is a key indicator of economic health
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines by >1% over 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in >40% of such instances over a 5-year period, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls signifies a healthy and expanding labor market, supporting consumer confidence and spending, which in turn drives corporate profitability and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:job growth is fundamental to economic expansion
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows consistent growth (e.g., >100k jobs/month for 6 months) but S&P 500 5-day returns are predominantly negative over a 2-year period, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is high and the Federal Reserve is actively raising interest rates, the combined effect of tighter monetary policy and eroding purchasing power creates a challenging environment for equities, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by >0.25% in a month AND CPIAUCSL annual growth > 4%
evidence_refs: fact:monetary tightening in inflationary periods typically hurts stocks
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS increases by >0.25% in a month and CPIAUCSL annual growth > 4%, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in >30% of such instances over a 3-year period, this hypothesis is falsified.
